text: IRAN CEASEFIRE DEMAND: Iran tells mediators it will only agree to ceasefire if US guarantees neither America nor Israel will ever strike Iran again. Tehran also demands compensation payments and right to full nuclear fuel cycle.

FBI ALERT: FBI warns California police departments that Iran 'aspired to conduct a surprise attack using drones from an unidentified vessel off the US West Coast.' Oscars security elevated for March 15 ceremony.

SPAIN WITHDRAWS AMBASSADOR: Spain permanently withdraws its ambassador to Israel over opposition to Iran war and Gaza. Trump accuses Spain of being 'unfriendly' after Madrid denied US access to military bases for Iran operations.

GREECE PRICE CAPS: Greece caps fuel and food profit margins for 3 months to prevent price gouging from Iran war. Gas stations limited to 12-cent/liter markup. Germany limits gas station price changes to once per day.

JOINT ATTACK: Iran and Hezbollah launch coordinated 'joint and integrated operation' on Israel. Hezbollah fires 150+ rockets at northern Israel while Iran launches ballistic missiles at central Israel. IDF begins 'large-scale' strikes on Beirut suburbs.

IEA EMERGENCY: International Energy Agency approves record 400 million barrel emergency oil reserve release — largest coordinated drawdown in history. 60-day release to stabilize markets as Hormuz remains effectively closed.

IRAN 37TH WAVE: Iran launches super-heavy Khoramshahr ballistic missiles in largest attack wave since war began. Targets include Israel, US bases in Gulf, and Saudi Arabia. IDF reports multiple interceptions; debris causes casualties.
HORMUZ: 3 more commercial ships attacked near Strait of Hormuz — total 14 vessels damaged/sunk since war began. Shipping traffic down 95% from pre-war levels. 320+ vessels at anchor.
TRUMP: 'There's practically nothing left to target' in Iran. Claims campaign nearly complete. Netanyahu pushes for continued strikes. US-Israel strategic divergence widens further.
LEBANON: Cabinet votes 22-2 to declare Hezbollah's military activities illegal — historic move to distance from Iran proxy. Hezbollah political wing remains legal. Could reshape Lebanon's future.

DEATH TOLL: Kurdish rights group Hengaw reports 4,300+ killed in Iran — triple the government's official count of 1,380. Internet blackout (4% capacity) makes independent verification nearly impossible.
FALSE CLAIM: Energy Secretary Chris Wright says gas prices have dropped during Iran war. AAA data shows prices UP from $3.18 to $3.578/gal since Feb 27. Diesel at $4.66/gal.
MARKETS: Oil rebounds +3.4% after yesterday's 11% crash. Brent at $90.77, WTI at $86.01. CPI at 2.4% (pre-war data). Dow -0.89%, S&P -0.33%. Goldman warns inflation could hit 3%+ if war continues.
HERITAGE: Isfahan UNESCO sites damaged by airstrikes — Naqsh-e Jahan Square, Shah Mosque, Chehel Sotoun palace, Ali Qapu hit. International condemnation mounts.
BREAKING: 40 killed in US-Israeli airstrike on residential buildings in eastern Tehran. Oil facility strikes blanket capital in toxic smoke.
PENTAGON: Hegseth warns tomorrow will be 'most intense day of strikes' against Iran since war began. CENTCOM confirms 3,000+ targets struck.
IRAN: Tehran vows 'eye for an eye' retaliation if US-Israel continues striking oil and energy infrastructure. Senior official says war enters 'new phase.'
DIPLOMACY: China, Russia, and France contact Iran regarding ceasefire. Iran rejects all mediation: 'No negotiations while under attack.'
ARAK: 5 killed in US-Israeli airstrike on residential building in western Iran. Saudi Arabia intercepts Iranian drone east of al-Kharj.
DAY 11: Turkey confirms NATO air defenses shot down Iranian ballistic missile entering Turkish airspace — second interception over Turkey this week
BREAKING: Bahrain reports 32 wounded including children in Iranian strike on Sitra; woman killed in separate missile hit on Manama residential area
DIPLOMACY: Lebanon proposes direct peace talks with Israel — both US and Israel reject offer, citing ongoing Iranian threat
PENTAGON: Hegseth says Iran has fired 500+ ballistic missiles and 2,000 drones since Feb 28 — 'Iranian Navy is largely no more'
ENERGY: US gas average hits $3.54/gal; diesel surges to $4.66/gal — up 89 cents in one week. Biggest weekly fuel price increase on record.
CONGRESS: House votes against war powers resolution 212-219, joining Senate 47-53 defeat. No Congressional check on Iran operations.
IRAN FM: Araghchi rejects ceasefire on NBC — 'We need to continue fighting for the sake of our people.' Says Iran will not negotiate under fire.
DAY 10: Trump says 'we've already won in many ways, but we haven't won enough.' Calls new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei a 'lightweight.' Says he must have a role in choosing Iran's next leader.
SENATE: War powers resolution to force Trump pullback from Iran FAILS in Senate vote. President authorized to continue strikes indefinitely.

DISINFORMATION: Fake AI-generated satellite imagery of US base in Qatar destroyed — spread across Instagram, Threads, X. AFP detected Google SynthID watermark. Millions of views in multiple languages.
IDF CHIEF warns there is 'no safe place' for the Iranian regime as war enters second week. Israel says Lebanon has made more attacks than Iran over past week.
DEVELOPING: Blast outside synagogue in Liege, Belgium. Investigation underway. Occurring amid heightened global tensions from Iran-Israel conflict.

HOUTHIS: IDF surprised Yemen's Houthis have NOT joined Iran war despite expectations. Houthi leader says 'finger on the trigger' but has not attacked. Analysts debate whether US involvement is deterring entry.
HUMANITARIAN: Iran's death toll surpasses 1,332 — over 6,000 wounded. At least 300 children hospitalized. Red Crescent reports escalating humanitarian crisis.
MARKETS: Oil smashes through $100/barrel — Strait of Hormuz effectively closed. Brent crude hits $119.50 intraday. Biggest weekly surge since 1983. 20% of world's oil supply threatened.
BREAKING: Mojtaba Khamenei named Iran's new Supreme Leader following father's assassination. Assembly of Experts elected son of slain Ayatollah as new leader. IRGC pledges allegiance.
DEVELOPING: Trump demands Iran's 'unconditional surrender' — Iran rejects ceasefire. FM Araghchi denies any behind-scenes contact with US.
UPDATE: Swiss government declares US-Israel attacks violate international law. Swiss Defense Minister says all parties broke prohibition on use of force.
UPDATE: 8th US service member killed in Iran war. Soldier dies from injuries at Saudi Arabia base. Trump attends dignified transfer at Dover AFB.
BREAKING: Saudi Arabia: 2 killed in first civilian deaths from Iranian strikes. Projectile hits residential area in Al-Kharj near Riyadh. 12 wounded.
BREAKING: Iranian drone damages Bahrain desalination plant, 1 dead. Hostile drone struck Salman Industrial City. First infrastructure attack on Bahrain.
DAY 8: Israel launches 'wide-scale' strikes on Tehran energy infrastructure. IDF targets regime energy resources in new phase of operations.
BREAKING: Hezbollah drone strikes British RAF base at Akrotiri, Cyprus. Runway damaged. Macron says 'attack on Cyprus is attack on all Europe.'
DAY 6: Israel launches new wave of strikes on Tehran - Iran responds with 9 waves of attacks on Jerusalem and southern Israel
BREAKING: Iranian UAVs strike Azerbaijan (Nakhchivan) for the first time - new theater in the conflict
DEVELOPING: Iran declares readiness for 'long-term war' - Hegseth says US 'just getting started'
UPDATE: US submarine sinks Iranian warship IRINS Dena off Sri Lanka - 101 of 180 sailors missing
MARKETS: $3.2 trillion in global equity lost in 96 hours - Brent crude $84/bbl - European gas +38% - Gold touches $5,400
DAY 7: B-2 stealth bombers hit Iranian underground nuclear facilities at Isfahan and Natanz with bunker-busting munitions - Pentagon confirms secondary explosions
BREAKING: Iran fires ballistic missiles at US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain - Pentagon confirms 12 US service members wounded, 3 in critical condition
MARKETS: Brent crude surges past $90/barrel - Goldman Sachs warns of $120 oil if Hormuz remains closed - massive cyber attack cripples Iranian banking system
DEVELOPING: UN Security Council emergency session ends with US-UK veto of Russia-China ceasefire resolution - anti-war protests erupt across major US cities
DAY 8: Israel targets Iranian oil infrastructure - massive fires at Tehran oil storage facility near key refinery
BREAKING: Trump says ground troops in Iran 'possible' for 'very good reason' - NBC reports private interest in deployment
UPDATE: Iran death toll surpasses 1,332 - CENTCOM says 3,000+ targets struck and 43 warships destroyed since Feb 28
MARKETS: WTI crude hits $90.90/bbl (+20.4% since pre-war) - US gas prices surge to $3.32/gal - biggest weekly oil gain in futures history
BREAKING: IRGC claims hits on oil tankers Prima and Louise P in Persian Gulf - 8+ vessels damaged since blockade began
DAY 9: US-Israel announce 'most intense bombing campaign yet' - Iran fires new missile salvo at Tel Aviv - explosions heard across city
UPDATE: Iran claims capture of US soldiers - Trump administration denies reports - Ali Larijani says US 'concealing captures'
HUMANITARIAN: UN estimates 330,000 displaced across Middle East - 28,000 Americans evacuated - Khamenei funeral postponed due to strikes
DEVELOPING: Iranian clerics close to selecting new Supreme Leader successor to Khamenei - Israel vows to target successor
BREAKING: Six vessels attacked in Gulf & Strait of Hormuz — Thai-flagged Mayuree Naree hit by projectiles, 3 crew missing; Japan-flagged ONE Majesty damaged. Total ships struck now 16
DEVELOPING: Iran explosive-laden boats attack two fuel tankers in Iraqi waters — Safesea Vishnu & Zefyros ablaze, 1 crew killed, 25 evacuated. Iraq shuts down oil port operations
CYBER: Iran-linked hackers claim cyberattack on US medical tech company Stryker — first significant hack on American company since war began
THREAT: Iran publishes target list of US tech giant offices — Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, Palantir, IBM, Oracle facilities in Israel & Gulf states threatened
STRIKE: Israel hits apartment in central Beirut's Aisha Bakkar area — seafront strike kills 7. Lebanon death toll rises to 634, 816K displaced
MARKETS: Oil surges — Brent $94.77/bbl, WTI $93.24/bbl after 6 vessels attacked. US Navy refuses merchant ship escort requests through Hormuz
